Healthy Living · Stronger Communities
To serve those who provide health to others
Elias Range Stays

10-Year Vision

Building the full-stack infrastructure layer for healthcare workforce housing in Canada.
Where We're Going
Not just a housing provider. An infrastructure layer.

In 10 years, ERS is the leading healthcare workforce housing platform in Canada. Hubs anchored to hospitals across BC and beyond, serving both sides of the Triangle: the construction companies who build them and the health authorities who staff them.

The Triangle
Three layers. One platform.

⬆ Equity Developments

Purpose-built hospital hub developments across BC and Canada. Mixed-use, community-anchored, nature-integrated.

Small Multifamily

ERS-managed properties near hospitals, serving mid-term and long-term housing needs.

🏗 Construction Contractors

Workforce housing for the crews who build the infrastructure — from site to completion.

🏥 Health Authorities

Dedicated housing for agency staff, traveling nurses, and locum physicians.

The Hub Model — Dawson Creek Phase 1
Our first purpose-built hub. Completion 2027.

10620 10th St, Dawson Creek, BC. The proof-of-concept that unlocks everything else.

32–40

Residential units — fully furnished, purpose-built for healthcare workforce.

Community

Community centre + daycare — designed for people staying weeks, not nights.

Wellness

Exercise facility + health clinic — infrastructure that supports the whole person.

0.7km

From the hospital — close enough to walk, far enough to leave work behind.

2035 — At Scale
The Dawson Creek hub unlocks everything else.
5+
Hub Locations
BC+
National Expansion
$113M+
Annual Run Rate

When a health authority sees a finished hub, the conversation shifts from "can you do this?" to "how fast can you scale?" The Dawson Creek hub is the proof point that unlocks everything else.

The Platform
Every hub is more than housing.

It's a community platform — connected to the natural landscapes, the local economy, the services and people around it. The Draw Close program. Long table dinners. Outdoor excursions. People who come for a posting and stay because they found a home.

"Not just less — MORE. More retention, more stability, more community health outcomes, more meaning for the people who show up every day to keep our hospitals open."

— Elias Range Stays
